candidates.description: The road surface shows aging concrete/asphalt with visible longitudinal cracks running along the pavement, particularly visible in images 2-4. No holes or depressions with missing asphalt are present - the surface is intact with surface-level cracking only. The area appears to be a residential street with well-maintained landscaping adjacent to the roadway.

The road surface shows aged concrete/asphalt with visible cracking patterns and surface wear. The pavement has multiple hairline to moderate cracks visible throughout, particularly in images 5-7 showing the southwest to northwest views. No holes with missing asphalt or exposed base material are visible - the damage is limited to surface cracking and general weathering.

debug_points.description: The road surface at this intersection in Panama City shows concrete pavement in generally good condition. Multiple angles reveal a well-maintained residential/commercial intersection with visible crosswalk markings, painted curbs, and landscaped median islands. No potholes, significant cracks, or surface damage are visible on the roadway.

The street-level images show a residential intersection in Panama City with paved asphalt roads in generally good condition. The road surface appears intact with no visible potholes, holes, or significant damage. There is normal wear and some minor surface texture variation but no missing asphalt or depressions.

The road surface shows aging concrete/asphalt with visible longitudinal cracks running along the pavement, particularly visible in images 2-4. No holes or depressions with missing asphalt are present - the surface is intact with surface-level cracking only. The area appears to be a residential street with well-maintained landscaping adjacent to the roadway.

The street view images show a residential street in Panama City with parked vehicles, landscaped medians with trees and flowering plants, and graffiti-covered corrugated metal fencing. The road surface visible in images 6 and 7 shows concrete/asphalt in fair condition with no potholes or significant damage visible. The greenish blur in the lower portions of several images appears to be a stitching artifact from the Street View camera, not actual road surface.

The street view images show a residential street in Panama City with parked vehicles, green grass median areas, and mature trees. The asphalt road surface appears to be in generally good condition with no visible potholes, significant cracks, or surface damage. There are fallen tree branches on the grass areas but these do not affect the road surface.

The street view images show a well-maintained residential street in what appears to be Panama City (Panama flag visible in image 5). The road surface is concrete/asphalt in good condition with no visible potholes, holes, or significant damage. The area features parked cars, landscaped grass medians, and appears to be a quiet residential neighborhood.

The street view images show a residential/urban area in Panama City with parked vehicles, green spaces, and paved roads. The road surfaces visible across all angles appear to be in generally good condition with normal asphalt texture. No potholes, significant cracks, or road damage are visible in any of the street-level images.

The street-level images show a residential street in what appears to be Panama City (based on coordinates and visible 'LALIQUE' signage). The road surface is asphalt in generally fair condition with normal wear patterns. Some debris and minor deterioration visible near curbs in image 8 (Northwest), but no discrete holes or missing asphalt material. The pavement shows typical aging but no potholes.

The road surface shows aged concrete/asphalt with visible cracking patterns and surface wear. The pavement has multiple hairline to moderate cracks visible throughout, particularly in images 5-7 showing the southwest to northwest views. No holes with missing asphalt or exposed base material are visible - the damage is limited to surface cracking and general weathering.

This location shows a well-maintained private driveway or parking area entrance with painted yellow directional arrows and 'NO SE ESTACIONE' (no parking) markings. The asphalt surface is in good condition with no visible potholes, holes, or significant damage. Yellow curb markings and traffic cones indicate organized traffic management.
